points.text: Research and Markets announced a 'Principles & Applications of CRISPR Training Course' scheduled for July 7, 2026, focusing on discovery, molecular mechanisms, and advantages over other genome editing tools.
The global DNA read, write, and edit market is projected to surge to $67.7 billion by 2030, driven explicitly by CRISPR advances, genomic diagnostics, and expanding clinical applications.
CRISPR Therapeutics priced $550 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private offering to qualified institutional buyers.
CRISPR Therapeutics granted initial purchasers an option to purchase up to an additional $50 million of notes, upsizing the offering from $350 million.
CRISPR Therapeutics established an effective coupon of 1.7308% on the notes to offset anticipated 35% Swiss tax withholding on interest payments.
The convertible notes carry an initial conversion rate of 13.0617 common shares per $1,000 principal amount, equivalent to a conversion price of approximately $76.56 per share.

The RUBY trial demonstrated that renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel), an AsCas12a-mediated CRISPR therapy disrupting BCL11A binding sites in HBG1/HBG2 promoters, normalized hemoglobin and eliminated vaso-occlusive events in severe sickle cell disease patients.

Research and Markets — CRISPR Training Course — Genome Editing Education — Online event scheduled for July 7, 2026; curriculum covers molecular mechanisms and comparative advantages over other genome editing tools.
CRISPR — Market Expansion — Gene Editing — Global market value projected to reach $67.7 billion by 2030, fueled by CRISPR technology advances and clinical adoption.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Convertible Senior Notes Offering — Capital Markets — Priced $550 million aggregate principal amount of 2031 notes with 1.7308% effective coupon; closing expected March 16, 2026.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Convertible Senior Notes — Capital Markets — $550M offering priced March 11, 2026; 1.7308% effective coupon; 13.0617 shares per $1,000 note; conversion price $76.56 (45% premium to $52.80 reference price); maturity March 1, 2031.

Hanna et al. — renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel) — Sickle Cell Disease — RUBY trial results show AsCas12a-mediated disruption of BCL11A binding sites in HBG1/HBG2 promoters drives deep, durable HbF reactivation and eliminates vaso-occlusive events.

Hanna et al. — renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel) — Sickle Cell Disease — AsCas12a-mediated direct disruption of BCL11A binding sites in HBG1/HBG2 promoters achieved deep, durable HbF reactivation and clinical quiescence in the RUBY trial.
Research and Markets — Educational service expansion — CRISPR Training Course — Offering online event on July 7, 2026 to address discovery and molecular mechanisms of CRISPR technology.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Capital raising for pipeline advancement — Convertible Senior Notes (2031) — Secured $550 million + $50 million option; initial conversion price set at $76.56 per share (45% premium to March 10, 2026 close).

CRISPR Therapeutics — Capital raising for CRISPR-based pipeline expansion — Convertible Senior Notes (2031) — $550M secured; 1.7308% coupon; 45% premium conversion price; funds to support clinical programs and operational needs.
CRISPR — Mechanism: Genome editing tool — Context: Training curriculum — Focus: Molecular mechanisms and comparative advantages over other genome editing tools; event date July 7, 2026.

renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel) — Mechanism: AsCas12a-mediated CRISPR disruption of BCL11A binding sites in HBG1/HBG2 promoters — Phase: Clinical (RUBY trial) — Population: Severe sickle cell disease — Comparator: Not specified in text — Results: Normalized hemoglobin, eliminated vaso-occlusive events, deep durable HbF reactivation.

reni-cel — Mechanism: AsCas12a-mediated direct disruption of BCL11A binding sites in HBG1/HBG2 promoters — Phase: RUBY trial — Population: Severe sickle cell disease — Comparator: N/A — Results: Normalized hemoglobin, eliminated vaso-occlusive events, robust HbF induction without impaired erythropoiesis.
CRISPR Therapeutics — Convertible Senior Notes Offering — $550 million principal amount due 2031; upsized from $350 million; effective coupon 1.7308%; conversion rate 13.0617 shares per $1,000.

CRISPR Therapeutics — Upsized convertible notes offering — $550M principal amount (initially $350M); 1.125% base coupon increased to 1.7308% to neutralize 35% Swiss withholding tax; settlement March 16, 2026; 13.0617 shares per $1,000 note.
